{"description":"Design and build Abacus: a standalone SQLite-backed graph tool that models an application's API surface, entities, pages, and user actions. Serves as the persistent, cumulative codebase understanding layer that bridges Gherkin specs to implementation. Standalone project (new Linear project: Abacus) with optional beads integration via hooks.

Linear issue: https://linear.app/pcom-mike/issue/ABA-1/brainstorm-abacus-living-application-graph-gherkin-driven-bdd

## Core Architecture
- SQLite database (.abacus/abacus.db)
- CLI tool (abacus or ab)
- MCP server for Claude Code integration
- Auto-scan scripts for structural layer
- Agent enrichment protocol for semantic layer

## Graph Node Types (Launch)
1. Routes — auto-scanned from oRPC contracts (method, path, input/output schemas)
2. Entities — auto-scanned from Prisma schema (models, fields, relations)
3. Pages — auto-scanned from React Router config (URL, components)
4. Actions — agent-enriched semantic bridge (gherkin_patterns, api_route, entities, page, permissions, ui_hints)
5. Permissions — hybrid (auto-scan ACL config + agent enrichment)

## The BDD Flow
Gherkin step → abacus actions --match → resolves to Action node → Action tells agent the API route, entities, page, and permissions → agent generates E2E test + implementation

## Population Strategy
- Hybrid: auto-scan + agent enrichment
- abacus scan rebuilds structural layer from code
- Agents enrich Actions as they work through features
- Actions are the cumulative knowledge — they persist and grow

## Beads Integration (optional)
- Hooks connect beads and abacus when both are present
- When a bead is worked on, agents can annotate which Actions/Routes were touched
- Graph data can feed into bead descriptions","priority":1,"title":"Brainstorm: Abacus — Living Application Graph + Gherkin-driven BDD"}

Serves as the persistent, cumulative codebase understanding layer that bridges Gherkin specs to implementation. Standalone project (new Linear project: Abacus) with optional beads integration via hooks.

Linear issue: https://linear.app/pcom-mike/issue/ABA-1/brainstorm-abacus-living-application-graph-gherkin-driven-bdd

## Core Architecture
- SQLite database (.abacus/abacus.db)
- CLI tool (abacus or ab)
- MCP server for Claude Code integration
- Auto-scan scripts for structural layer
- Agent enrichment protocol for semantic layer

## Graph Node Types (Launch)
1. Routes — auto-scanned from oRPC contracts (method, path, input/output schemas)
2. Entities — auto-scanned from Prisma schema (models, fields, relations)
3. Pages — auto-scanned from React Router config (URL, components)
4. Actions — agent-enriched semantic bridge (gherkin_patterns, api_route, entities, page, permissions, ui_hints)
5. Permissions — hybrid (auto-scan ACL config + agent enrichment)

## The BDD Flow
Gherkin step → abacus actions --match → resolves to Action node → Action tells agent the API route, entities, page, and permissions → agent generates E2E test + implementation

## Population Strategy
- Hybrid: auto-scan + agent enrichment
- abacus scan rebuilds structural layer from code
- Agents enrich Actions as they work through features
- Actions are the cumulative knowledge — they persist and grow

## Beads Integration (optional)
- Hooks connect beads and abacus when both are present
- When a bead is worked on, agents can annotate which Actions/Routes were touched
- Graph data can feed into bead descriptions","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mike@seedless.club","created_at":"2026-03-06T20:19:44Z","created_by":"mjn","updated_at":"2026-03-06T20:20:59Z"}

{"id":"aba-zca","title":"Abacus: Living Application Graph + Gherkin-driven BDD","description":"Build Abacus: a standalone SQLite-backed graph tool that models an application's API surface, entities, pages, and user actions. Serves as the persistent, cumulative codebase understanding layer that bridges Gherkin specs to implementation.

Linear project: Abacus (pcom-mike workspace)
Linear issue: https://linear.app/pcom-mike/issue/ABA-1/brainstorm-abacus-living-application-graph-gherkin-driven-bdd

## Architecture

- **Database**: SQLite (.abacus/abacus.db) per target project, WAL mode, JSONB properties, FTS5
- **Monorepo**: pnpm workspaces — packages/core, packages/cli, packages/mcp-server
- **Graph Model**: Adjacency list (nodes + edges tables) with 5 node types:
  - Routes (auto-scanned from oRPC contracts)
  - Entities (auto-scanned from Prisma schema)
  - Pages (auto-scanned from React Router config)
  - Actions (agent-enriched semantic bridge)
  - Permissions (hybrid: auto-scan + enrichment)
- **Scanner Architecture**: Pluggable Scanner interface with implementations for oRPC, Prisma, React Router
- **Match Algorithm**: Tiered — Cucumber expressions → FTS5 fuzzy → LLM-assisted Action creation
- **MCP Server**: @modelcontextprotocol/server via stdio transport
- **CLI**: Commander.js + @commander-js/extra-typings

## Technology Stack
- TypeScript (strict, ES2022, bundler moduleResolution)
- pnpm 10+, Node 24+
- better-sqlite3 for database
- ts-morph for AST parsing (oRPC, Router)
- @prisma/internals getDMMF for Prisma schema
- @cucumber/gherkin + @cucumber/expression for Gherkin parsing & matching
- @modelcontextprotocol/server for MCP
- Zod v4 for validation

## Key Decisions
1. Standalone tool, not embedded in beads — optional hooks integration
2. .abacus/ directory lives in the TARGET project (like .beads/)
3. Graph schema is API-first with Action semantic layer
4. Full BDD cycle: Gherkin → E2E test → implementation
5. Match algorithm is a GROWTH mechanism — LLM fallback creates new Actions
6. Scanners are pluggable from day 1

## Phases
1. Foundation (scaffolding, schema, scanner arch)
2. Scanners (oRPC, Prisma, Router — parallelizable)
3. Core Features (query layer, actions, match algorithm)
4. Interfaces (CLI commands, MCP server)
5. Integration (E2E validation against hourly codebase)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mike@seedless.club","created_at":"2026-03-06T20:49:55Z","created_by":"mjn","updated_at":"2026-03-06T20:49:55Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"Build Abacus: a standalone SQLite-backed graph tool that models an application's API surface, entities, pages, and user actions. Serves as the persistent, cumulative codebase understanding layer that bridges Gherkin specs to implementation.

Linear project: Abacus (pcom-mike workspace)
Linear issue: https://linear.app/pcom-mike/issue/ABA-1/brainstorm-abacus-living-application-graph-gherkin-driven-bdd

## Architecture

- **Language**: Go (single binary, excellent compiler feedback, matches beads tooling)
- **Database**: SQLite (.abacus/abacus.db) per target project via modernc.org/sqlite (pure Go, no CGo)
- **CLI**: cobra (same as beads)
- **MCP Server**: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk (official Go SDK)
- **Graph Model**: Adjacency list (nodes + edges tables) with JSONB properties, FTS5 search
- **Node Types**: Route, Entity, Page, Action, Permission

## BYOASTP (Bring Your Own AST Parser) Scanner Architecture

Abacus does NOT parse source code itself. Instead:
- Abacus defines a **Scanner Protocol** — a JSON schema for scan results
- Scanners are **standalone executables** that output JSON conforming to the protocol
- Abacus spawns scanners as subprocesses, reads their JSON output, ingests into SQLite
- Config (.abacus/config.yaml) maps scanner IDs to executable commands

This means:
- Abacus core is 100% Go — no TypeScript, no AST parsing, no language-specific deps
- Scanners can be written in ANY language (Node.js for TS, Python for Django, etc.)
- We ship 4 reference scanners as npm packages (oRPC, Prisma, React Router, Express)
- Community can write scanners for any stack

## Project Structure
\`\`\`
abacus/
├── go.mod / go.sum
├── cmd/abacus/main.go           # CLI + MCP server entry point
├── internal/
│   ├── db/                      # SQLite connection, schema, migrations
│   ├── graph/                   # GraphRepository, ActionService
│   ├── match/                   # MatchService, CucumberMatcher, FuzzyMatcher, SuggestionBuilder
│   ├── scanner/                 # Scanner protocol types, subprocess runner
│   └── cli/                     # Cobra command definitions
├── mcp/                         # MCP server tool/resource registration
├── scanners/                    # Reference scanner implementations (Node.js)
│   ├── orpc/                    # @abacus/scanner-orpc
│   ├── prisma/                  # @abacus/scanner-prisma
│   ├── react-router/            # @abacus/scanner-react-router
│   └── express/                 # @abacus/scanner-express
└── testdata/                    # Test fixtures
\`\`\`

## Technology Stack
- Go 1.23+ (single binary distribution)
- modernc.org/sqlite (pure Go SQLite, no CGo)
- github.com/spf13/cobra (CLI framework)
- github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk (MCP server)
- github.com/cucumber/cucumber-expressions-go (pattern matching)
- github.com/cucumber/gherkin/go (Gherkin parsing)
- Node.js scanner packages: ts-morph, @prisma/internals, @cucumber/gherkin

## Key Decisions
1. Go for core — single binary, great compiler messages, matches beads ecosystem
2. BYOASTP — scanners are standalone executables, not embedded parsers
3. .abacus/ directory lives in the TARGET project (like .beads/)
4. Scanner Protocol is the interface — JSON in/out, language-agnostic
5. Match algorithm is a GROWTH mechanism — LLM fallback creates new Actions
6. 4 reference scanners ship as npm packages for the hourly stack
7. Official Go MCP SDK for Claude Code integration

## Phases
1. Foundation (scaffolding, schema, scanner protocol)
2. Scanners (oRPC, Prisma, Router, Express — Node.js, parallelizable)
3. Core Features (query layer, actions, match algorithm)
4. Interfaces (CLI commands, MCP server)
5. Integration (E2E validation against hourly codebase)"}
